3
Case Study
MODULE THREE –Seed LAUNCH case study – Kami McBride
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Launch Summary
▪ 	Kami McBride – For almost 30 years, Kami has been teaching people to grow and
use herbs in their daily lives for cooking, gardening, skin care and stress reduction.
She works to de-mystify the world of herbal medicine, and loves helping people
learn how to use herbs in the home setting for prevention and every day common
ailments.
▪ 	Email list: approximately 6,500 (built from teaching at workshops, conferences
and colleges)
▪ 	Market/Niche: Health and wellness, family wellness, children’s wellness, Mom’s
wanting to use natural remedies to take care of their family
▪ 	Product Name Created during Seed Launch: Handcrafted Healing Herbal
Oils - The simple, homemade solution for non-toxic natural skin care and the family
friendly remedy for dozens of common aches and pains, from burns and bruises to
eczema and muscle pain
▪ 	Offer price: $97
▪ 	Sales: 104 people purchased course with 0 refunds
▪ 	Total revenue: $10,088
▪ 	Program Delivery: My courses are hands-on demonstrations, so I pre-recorded 3
videos before the class started so people would have something to begin with. I would
release a few videos and then take a survey.
After each survey I organized all of the questions and then did a live call answering the
questions that were submitted and also taking live questions and comments on the calls.
I took much longer to deliver the course than Jeff talks about because people wanted
more and I kept making specific lessons that addressed all the questions. This seed
launch took place from the beginning of May through the end of July. People gave me
feedback that they wanted to take breaks and felt behind, so we took catch up breaks.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 17 --

4
Case Study
MODULE THREE –Seed LAUNCH case study – Kami McBride
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Because of the survey process I created a course that was much more of what people
wanted. Because of all the feedback, I feel like this is a much better course than what I
could have made on my own.
Having taught this material for so long, I was definitely in the wrong mindset, I thought
I already knew everything that people wanted. I was actually REALLY surprised by
how much I learned about what people wanted and needed. I am glad I learned this
lesson now instead of creating a bunch of courses without the feedback that you get
during a seed launch.
Now I am of the mindset that I wouldn’t create a new product or book WITHOUT a seed
launch!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 17 --

5
Case Study
MODULE THREE –Seed LAUNCH case study – Kami McBride
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Communication Strategy
Driving Traffic
Sent email broadcasts to list, posted on Facebook (no Facebook ads, since it was a seed
launch) and Pinterest. Also, found that writing blog posts that have key words/course title in
them help to bring the course name up in a google search. The blog posts also are a big help in
driving Pinterest traffic to the launch:
http://livingawareness.com/5-healing-uses-st-johns-wort-oil/
http://livingawareness.com/handcrafted-healing-herbal-oils/
Prelaunch EMAIL Strategy
1. 	Prelaunch email #1
▪ 	( I mention that I am working on a new course)
▪ 	Email subject title: What do you call your medicine cabinet?
▪ 	23.46% open rate/ 3.25% Click through rate
2. 	Prelaunch email #2 (Or PLC #1)
▪ 	Email subject title: How to Make Lavender infused Oil (video)
▪ 	Sent out 9 days after Prelaunch email #1
▪ 	24.83% open rate/ 6.26% Click through rate
▪ 	This broadcast took people to a blog post, but really the blog post video acted like a PLC 1
3. 	Prelaunch email #3
▪ 	Email subject title: Tell me what you think!
▪ 	Sent out 6 days after Prelaunch email #2
▪ 	26.2% open rate/ 4.8% Click through rate
▪ 	Included link to survey
▪ 	109 people responded to this survey with AWESOME information
4. 	Prelaunch email #4
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

▪ 	Email subject title: VIDEO: Answers to your questions and making herbal oils that don't
go rancid
▪ 	Sent out 5 days after Prelaunch email #3
▪ 	23.8% open rate/ 4.5% Click through rate
5. 	Prelaunch email #5 sent to un-opens
▪ 	Email subject title: Video tutorial for advanced technique calendula infused herbal oil
▪ 	Sent out 2 days after previous email
▪ 	11.4% open rate/ 2.4% Click through rate
▪ 	246 people total opted in to the PLC emails
6. 	Open Cart email #1
▪ 	Email subject title: We’re open: Handcrafted Healing Herbal Oils registration…
▪ 	Sent out 2 days after PLC un-opens
▪ 	Sent to Launch List of 246: 66% open/ 32.93% click through
▪ 	Sent to entire list: 21.6% open/ 5.44% click through
7. 	Open Cart email #2
▪ 	Email subject title: ONLY ONCE A YEAR… can this essential home remedy be made!
Here's why…
▪ 	Sent 2 days after last email
▪ 	DITCHED the launch list and just sent to everyone on my list
▪ 	24.8% open rate/ 5.03% Click through rate
8. 	Open Cart email #3 to un-opens
▪ 	Sent same email, just changed title
▪ 	Email subject title: What's the big deal about St. John's wort?
▪ 	Sent 2 days after last email
▪ 	DITCHED the launch list and just sent to everyone on my list
▪ 	11.5% open rate/ 1.8% Click through rate
9. 	Open Cart email #4
▪ 	Email subject title: Want to ask me questions about Handcrafted Healing Herbal Oils?

8
Case Study
MODULE THREE –Seed LAUNCH case study – Kami McBride
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
EXAMPLE EMAILS – Prelaunch
PreLaunch email #1 (Pre-Prelaunch)
Email subject title: What do you call your medicine cabinet?
________________
Hello!
The Earth has awakened around here! I have about 50 baby calendula plants sprouting! My
St. John’s wort plants have come back doubling the amount of ground they covered last year
and the lavender has done nothing short of gone crazy, it wants to take over the whole garden
I think….
Anyway what that all means is it’s going to be a good herbal oil making year! (Which, by the
way, I am working on an awesome herbal oils class for you right now!)
Thanks to everyone who gave us leads for local herb shops, we will be publishing that list
soon!
Ok, so now I have a serious question, please consider this with me.
What do you call your medicine cabinet? The one that’s full of all your jars and
tubes of natural remedies?
We are normalizing this home art as part of who we are and what we do. We are reclaiming
home herbalism as just a regular thing that happens day to day in our homes as part of our
culture and how we raise our kids. (Definitely not what was happening when I was growing
up).
So part of reclaiming this craft is using language that reflects this home culture shift.
I kind of like Wellness Cabinet, but it doesn’t quite roll off the tongue like “Medicine Cabinet’
does….
I mean what will you actually say? I am trying to come up with something that people will
really use and say….
“Hey honey, go get some salve from the wellness cabinet, please?”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Surveys Sent during course creation
Each module has several lessons. So I would do the first two video lessons in a module, ask for
feedback, then do the rest of the lessons in the module.
Survey #1
Handcrafted Healing Herbal Oils Module #1 Survey
Lavender and Comfrey Lessons
Some people in this training have some herbal experience, some of you are starting from the
very beginning.
Either way, I need to know your top questions so I can make sure that everything is covered in
this training….. so please, give me your feedback below…..
Thanks,
1. What are your top questions about the Lavender and Comfrey lessons?
2. What other comments, insights and questions do you have about these Module 1 lessons or
about this course?
Please enter your email address below
Email Address
Submit
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Survey #2
Hi Handcrafters!
Ok, you have lots of examples, details and tutorials on how to make your oils! If you have any
final questions on Module 1 or how to make your oils, I am all ears!
What do you think about the St. John’s wort lesson!? I was so happy to get that to you early!
I am preparing your How to Grow St. John’s wort audio bonus. If you have any questions about
growing St. John’s wort, please ask them here so I can make sure that I cover everything you
need!
Looking forward to getting into the details of what to use the oils for and how to use them as we
move along in class!
1. Do you have any questions about making St. John’s wort oil?
2. Do you have any questions about growing St. John’s wort?
3. We are about ready to move on from Module 1. Do you have any last questions, ideas or
comments about Module 1, the ‘Make It’ Module?
4. How is class going for you?

Survey #3
Handcrafted Healing Herbal Oils Survey
OK…..we’ve covered a lot of examples on how to make your infused herbal oils in Module 1. Do
you have any other questions about making your oil?
I am deep into designing Module 2 – and this is where you’ll get to know your oils more in depth
and this module will help you understand what the oils can be used for.
You might have noticed this already, the survey only lets you fill it out once, sorry about that, we
can’t change the survey software. Try to get all your questions written down and into the survey.
However, if something else comes up, you can always ask more questions here:
support@livingawareness.com
✿✿ATTENTION✿✿: I am preparing your How to Grow St. John’s wort audio bonus. If you have
any questions about growing St. John’s wort, please ask them here so I can make sure that I
cover everything you need!
Thanks for being here with me on this journey!
1. Do you have any other questions about making your herbal oils?
2. Now I need to know the questions you have about using your oils.
What are your top two questions about using herbal oils?
3. Have you made an oil yet? I would love to hear about it, how did it go?
4. Please tell me any questions you have about growing St. John’s wort

Survey #8
Dear Handcrafters!
I would love to hear a story about any part of your journey with this course.
If we choose your story to share, as a thank you gift, I will be happy to jump on the phone with
you for a FREE 30 minute consultation about anything herbal of your choice and also send
you a free copy of my book, The Herbal Kitchen.
1. What was the best part of this course? What were you most excited about?
2. Can you tell me a story about something that happened for you in real life when you used an
herbal oil that you made?
3. How did this course benefit you?
Here is an exercise you can use if you need help getting started thinking about how you
benefited:
Before taking this course I knew this about herbal oils ________
And felt this ________
And today I know this ________
And feel this ________
4. If someone asked you what this course did for you, what specific things would you tell them
that it helped you with?
5. If you were recommending this course to a friend, what would you say?
6. Is there anything else you would like to add, anything you would tell someone else
considering this program?
7. Are there any questions that you still have about this course or any challenges with making or
using your herbal oils?
May I use your feedback as a testimonial to inspire others to bring the healing power of herbal
oils into their home wellness tool kit?
